title: opilia campestris var. strobilifera
intro: Opilia campestris var. strobilifera, also known as the Opilia or Opal plant, is a type of flowering plant native to India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Opilia campestris var. strobilifera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
